original_abstract: This review examines recent and noteworthy developments in the chemical preparation of chiral nanographenes, a compelling class of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) that combine the structural elegance of inherent chirality with the significant optical, electronic, and magnetic potential of graphitic units. While nanographene synthesis has traditionally relied heavily on the Scholl oxidation, the ubiquity of that method in recent literature warrants a dedicated look at alternative strategies. Consequently, this work focuses primarily on chiral architectures—including twistacenes, helicenes, imide-functionalized systems, and irregular non-alternant frameworks—prepared via non-Scholl methods such as alkyne-based benzannulations, metal-catalyzed cycloadditions, and radical cascades. By highlighting these unique and often under-reviewed synthetic pathways, we aim to illustrate how rational design can be used to fine-tune the chiroptical and electronic properties essential for the next generation of nanoelectronic applications.
humanized_abstract: Chemical synthesis of chiral nanographenes has been an active area of research in carbon materials science for the past several years. Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs), known as chiral nanographenes, are a particular class of carbon materials that share a common core of intrinsic chirality inherent in their structure, while offering the opportunity to fully exploit the outstanding optical, electronic, and magnetic properties inherent to graphitic structures. The overwhelming number of recent communications and full articles dealing with nanographene chemistry that rely almost exclusively to the Scholl oxidation calls for a critical survey of the synthesis of chiral nanographenes (such as twistacenes, helicenes or imide-functionalized nanographenes, and non-symmetrical non-alternant) by the less commonly used chemical routes and methods, including alkyne-based benzannulations, transition metal-promoted cycloadditions and radical cascades. Based on recent examples of design of new chiroptical and electronic properties towards the realization of future-generation nanomaterials and nanoelectronic devices, we show here an overview of the less often reported synthetic routes toward chiral nanographenes.
